WinTmnl.ASSOC: Displays or modifies file extension associations
WinTmnl.ATTRIB: Displays or changes file attributes
WinTmnl.BCDEDIT: Sets properties in boot database to control boot loading
WinTmnl.BREAK: Sets or clears extended CTRL+C checking
WinTmnl.CACLS: Displays or modifies access control lists (ACLs) of files
WinTmnl.CALL: Calls one batch program from another
WinTmnl.CD: Displays the name of or changes the current directory
WinTmnl.CHCP: Displays or sets the active code page number
WinTmnl.CHDIR: Displays the name of or changes the current directory
WinTmnl.CHKDSK: Checks a disk and displays a status report
WinTmnl.CHKNTFS: Displays or modifies the checking of disk at boot time
WinTmnl.CMD: Starts a new instance of the Windows command interpreter
WinTmnl.COLOR: Sets the default console foreground and background colors
WinTmnl.COMP: Compares the contents of two files or sets of files
WinTmnl.COMPACT: Displays or alters the compression of files on NTFS partitions
WinTmnl.CONVERT: Converts FAT volumes to NTFS.  You cannot convert the current drive
WinTmnl.COPY: Copies one or more files to another location
WinTmnl.DATE: Displays or sets the date
WinTmnl.DEL: Deletes one or more files
WinTmnl.DIR: Displays a list of files and subdirectories in a directory
WinTmnl.DISKPART: Displays or configures Disk Partition properties
WinTmnl.DRIVERQUERY: Displays current device driver status and properties
WinTmnl.ENDLOCAL: Ends localization of environment changes in a batch file
WinTmnl.ERASE: Deletes one or more files
WinTmnl.EXIT: Quits the CMD.EXE program (command interpreter)
WinTmnl.FC: Compares two files or sets of files
WinTmnl.FIND: Searches for a text string in a file or files
WinTmnl.FINDSTR: Searches for strings in files
WinTmnl.FOR: Runs a specified command for each file in a set of files
WinTmnl.FORMAT: Formats a disk for use with Windows
WinTmnl.FSUTIL: Displays or configures the file system properties
WinTmnl.FTYPE: Displays or modifies file types used in file extension associations
WinTmnl.GOTO: Directs the Windows command interpreter to a labeled line in a batch program
WinTmnl.GPRESULT: Displays Group Policy information for machine or user
WinTmnl.HELP: Provides Help information for Windows commands
WinTmnl.IF: Performs conditional processing in batch programs
WinTmnl.LABEL: Creates; changes; or deletes the volume label of a disk
WinTmnl.MKLINK: Creates Symbolic Links and Hard Link
WinTmnl.MORE: Displays output one screen at a time
WinTmnl.MOVE: Moves one or more files from one directory to another directory
WinTmnl.OPENFILES: Displays files opened by remote users for a file share
WinTmnl.PATH: Displays or sets a search path for executable files
WinTmnl.PAUSE: Suspends processing of a batch file and displays a message
WinTmnl.POPD: Restores the previous value of the current directory saved by PUSHD
WinTmnl.PROMPT: Changes the Windows command prompt
WinTmnl.PUSHD: Saves the current directory then changes it
WinTmnl.RECOVER: Recovers readable information from a bad or defective disk
WinTmnl.REM: Records comments (remarks) in batch files or CONFIG.SYS
WinTmnl.REN: Renames a file or files
WinTmnl.RENAME: Renames a file or files
WinTmnl.ROBOCOPY: Advanced utility to copy files and directory trees
WinTmnl.SC: Displays or configures services (background processes)
WinTmnl.SCHTASKS: Schedules commands and programs to run on a computer
WinTmnl.SETLOCAL: Begins localization of environment changes in a batch file
WinTmnl.SHIFT: Shifts the position of replaceable parameters in batch files
WinTmnl.SHUTDOWN: Allows proper local or remote shutdown of machine
WinTmnl.START: Starts a separate window to run a specified program or command
WinTmnl.SUBST: Associates a path with a drive letter
WinTmnl.SYSTEMINFO: Displays machine specific properties and configuration
WinTmnl.TASKKILL: Kill or stop a running process or application
WinTmnl.TASKLIST: Displays all currently running tasks including services
WinTmnl.TIME: Displays or sets the system time
WinTmnl.TITLE: Sets the window title for a CMD.EXE session
WinTmnl.TREE: Graphically displays the directory structure of a drive or path
WinTmnl.TYPE: Displays the contents of a text file
WinTmnl.VERIFY: Tells Windows whether to verify that your files are written correctly to a disk
WinTmnl.VOL: Displays a disk volume label and serial number
WinTmnl.WMIC: Displays WMI information inside interactive command shell
WinTmnl.XCOPY: Copies files and directory trees
